label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me think about this step by step. You start at a point. Let's call this point A. 1. First, you take 7 steps in a straight line. So you move from point A to point B, which is 7 steps away. 2. Then you turn around. Turning around means you face the opposite direction from which you came. So now you're facing back towards point A. 3. Then you take 7 steps again. Since you turned around, these 7 steps would take you back from point B to point A. Therefore, if you start at a point, take 7 steps, turn around, and take 7 steps, you end up back at the starting point. So the answer is "Yes". I should double-check: Taking 7 steps in one
